## User preferences

- when the user said "stop, and understand exactly what we have" and "do whatever it takes to really stop in this moment and give me the full picture" -> default similar sql-hidden-cost work to a broad architecture-and-framing pass, not a narrow fix [Task 1]
- when the user asked "Tell me the system prompt" and whether the layers "speak the same language" -> explicitly compare prompt surfaces, naming drift, and contradictory assumptions across layers instead of summarizing only one prompt source [Task 1][Task 2]
- when the user framed the repo as long-running regression / patch-on-patch drift -> prioritize truth-mapping, current state, and conflict detection over implementation optimism [Task 1]
- when the user asked what autonomous behavior "might be broken" -> separate proof-gate correctness from actual autonomy instead of treating a passed gate as end-to-end confidence [Task 3]

## Reusable knowledge

- There is no repo-owned root `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, or hidden system prompt here; the current benchmark prompt is a CLI prompt string passed to `codex exec`, plus generated workspace files such as `AGENTS.md` and `README.md` [Task 1][Task 2]
- `package.json` splits the repo into `proof:*`, `instrumented:*`, and `benchmark:*` families; `benchmark:all` is scoring/gating/presentation, not agent execution [Task 1]
- AST-Bench V1 currently defines 25 tasks, 2 lanes, 3 agents, 3 repeats, and 450 required lane runs, while `benchmark/results/summary.json` still says `case-study` with 3 captured/passed lane runs and 447 missing, so this repo is not yet at `public-v1` proof breadth [Task 1]
- Generated AST-Bench workspaces are symmetric in shape but intentionally asymmetric in data modeling: MongoDB uses document-shaped collections while Postgres uses normalized relational tables [Task 1]
- The current AST-Bench prompt says "You are running AST-Bench, the Agent Schema Tax Benchmark" with task title, business request, acceptance criteria, native-model rule, `npm test`, and "Do not hand-wave or simulate evidence", while the older seed prompt still says "MongoDB's Interactive Proof Lab" and bakes in the Customer 360 Escalation task [Task 2]
- Generated `AGENTS.md` files are terse task-local constraints rather than the authoritative prompt, and public fairness language in docs is stricter than the prompt string alone [Task 2]
- `check-proof-gates` verifies evidence-manifest SHA-256 integrity, `promote-verified-replay` enforces the verified replay contract, and `build-public-evidence-bundle` packages those artifacts; none of those scripts independently prove that current autonomous execution is healthy [Task 3]

## Failures and how to do differently

- Symptom: the repo gets treated like a DeepAgents/LangGraph runtime; cause: benchmark scripts and generated workspaces are mistaken for an in-repo agent runtime; fix: start from `package.json`, `scripts/benchmark-run.mjs`, `scripts/benchmark-prepare.mjs`, and the generated targets before reasoning about autonomy [Task 1]
- Symptom: AST-Bench results look like agent failures before workflow logic is even exercised; cause: generated acceptance tests leak `db` / `tables` globals that do not exist in test scope; fix: repair the harness/test generator before drawing autonomy conclusions, and treat `globalThis.db = db` workarounds as benchmark leakage rather than clean feature wins [Task 1]

- Symptom: a safe fix gets mixed with unrelated branch work; cause: the root workspace is not the deployed fix checkout; fix: do the safety patch in the clean worktree and keep the dirty main checkout reserved for the parallel branch only [Task 1][Task 2]
- Symptom: a follow-up agent merges away the production fix; cause: the handoff names only one branch or omits the protected invariants; fix: explicitly state both branches/worktrees, the live commit SHA, and the invariants that must not be reverted [Task 2]

## What I implemented

The single stub `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` in `src/workflow.mjs` — the only source change. It reads the workflow request, scopes records to the account + activity trail, then persists the document-native state and returns the portal projection via the existing `buildPortalView`.

**Persisted (keyed to `requestId: "audit-data-access-"`):**
- **`workflow_state`** — one record with status set to the request's expected outcome (the customer-facing `"Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail."`), the 6 risk signals, `nextStep`, approvers, and scoped record count.
- **`owner_tasks`** — one per owner group (Compliance, Legal, Security, Operations, in order), each with a title, a `dueAt` one day out from `now`, and `status: "open"`.
- **`customer_messages`** — one customer-safe message body.
- **`audit_events`** — one customer-visible event carrying a real `sha256` hash of its payload (the "hashable audit trail" the status promises — not simulated).

**Honoring the rules:**
- Tests, fixture data, and `portal-view.mjs` untouched.
- Model stayed MongoDB document-shaped (native collection pushes, `_id`-keyed lookups), no relational restructuring.
- Smallest production-style change: only the one stubbed function was filled in.

All derived acceptance fields (owner routing `"Compliance + Legal + Security + Operations"`, `riskSummary` = `"6 signals: ..."`, `"4 owner tasks"`, `"Audit visible"`) fall out naturally from the persisted records.

## What I implemented

- **`workflow_state`** — one record with status set to the request's expected outcome (the customer-facing `"Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail."`), the 6 risk signals, `nextStep`, approvers, and scoped record count.
- **`owner_tasks`** — one per owner group (Compliance, Legal, Security, Operations, in order), each with a title, a `dueAt` one day out from `now`, and `status: "open"`.
- **`customer_messages`** — one customer-safe message body.
- **`audit_events`** — one customer-visible event carrying a real `sha256` hash of its payload (the "hashable audit trail" the status promises — not simulated).

**Honoring the rules:**

22	strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
23	strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");

30	function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
31	  
32	  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
33	  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
34	  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
35	  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
36	  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");
37	  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
38	  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
39	  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
40	  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.dueAt && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
41	  strictEqual(db.customer_messages.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
42	  strictEqual(db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
43	}

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/split-shipment-exception/claude-code/repeat-1/mongo/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:44:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_015se9wxyqmVFbxq2ckQPjwf","type":"tool_result","content":"1	export function buildPortalView(db) {
2	  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
3	  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
4	  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
5	  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
6	  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
7	
8	  return {
9	    taskId: request.taskId,
10	    title: state?.title || request.title,
11	    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
12	    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
13	    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
14	    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
15	    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
16	    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
17	    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
18	  };
19	}
20	"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01QJr9pE3CUqGtgrgHfzFY1Z","session_id":"3da4c953-b9cf-4123-a695-9035bf6a8517","uuid":"9db4492b-8788-4adf-8a3b-625102154182","timestamp":"2026-06-22T23:30:42.012Z","subagent_type":"worker","task_description":"Explore codebase structure"}

## 1. Directory Structure

```
workspace/
├── AGENTS.md                                  (target rules)
├── README.md                                  (run instructions)
├── package.json
├── tests-before.log                           (captured failing-test output)

├── migrations/

│   ├── render-proof.mjs                        (SVG/JSON artifact generator)
│   └── workflow.mjs                            (THE FILE TO IMPLEMENT — currently a stub)
└── tests/
    └── acceptance.test.mjs                     (the contract)
```

There is **no `node_modules`** directory and **no lockfile**. No jest/mongo config files exist.

## 2. package.json

ESM project (`"type": "module"`). No dependencies at all — pure Node built-ins.
- `npm test` → `node tests/acceptance.test.mjs`
- `npm run render` → `node src/render-proof.mjs`

## 3. How MongoDB is used in tests — IMPORTANT

**There is no real MongoDB, no `mongodb-memory-server`, and no mocks.** Despite being branded a "MongoDB lane," the data is a plain JSON file (`data/collections.json`) loaded with `readFileSync` and `JSON.parse`. The "collections" are just arrays of plain JS objects on an in-memory `db` object. `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` mutates that in-memory object, and the test asserts against the mutated arrays. The MongoDB MCP server / migration file are decorative context, not used by the test runner. **Do not import the `mongodb` driver or connect to a cluster** — everything is synchronous in-memory object manipulation.

## 4. The data model (`data/collections.json`)

A single root object with these keys (each is the "collection"):
- `benchmark_fixture` — object: `{_id, suiteId, taskId, generatedAt}`
- `accounts` — array[1]: account `acct-split-ship` with nested `contract`, `contacts[]`, `context`
- `workflow_requests` — array[1]: **the driver record** (details below)
- `activities` — array[6]: signal activity log entries
- `workflow_state` — **empty array `[]`** (you must push to it)
- `owner_tasks` — **empty array `[]`** (you must push to it)
- `audit_events` — **empty array `[]`** (you must push to it)
- `customer_messages` — **empty array `[]`** (you must push to it)

The single `workflow_requests[0]` record (this drives all expected values):
```
_id:            "order-split-shipme"     ← used as requestId for all persisted records
taskId:         "split-shipment-exception"
accountId:      "acct-split-ship"
title:          "Split-shipment exception"
primaryEntity:  "order"
businessPrompt: "Implement a split-shipment exception workflow..."
expectedOutcome:"Split-shipment exception active with replacement plan, owners, customer message, and audit trail."
ownerGroups:    ["Operations", "Support", "Finance", "Logistics"]   (length 4)
riskSignals:    [ {name,detail} x6 ]  names in order:
                  "split", "shipment", "exception",
                  "enterprise account", "open customer impact", "audit required"
nextStep:       "Operations owner review by 16:00"
customerMessage:"Split-shipment exception is being handled by Operations, Support, Finance, Logistics."
```
Note: `workflow_requests[0]` does NOT have a `taskId`-vs-`taskId` distinction issue, but it has **no `taskId` field on the portal view path** — wait, it does have `taskId`. The portal view reads `request.taskId` for `taskId`. (There is no `request.taskId` collision concern.)

## 5. Source files (verbatim)

### `src/workflow.mjs` (THE STUB TO IMPLEMENT)
```js
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
```

### `src/portal-view.mjs` (read-side — already complete, defines the projection shape)
```js
export function buildPortalView(db) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

  return {
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```
This tells you exactly what `applyBenchmarkTask` must persist so that a subsequent `buildPortalView(db)` returns the expected projection. The implementation should mutate `db` (push records into the empty arrays) and then return `buildPortalView(db)`.

## 6. Test file (verbatim) — `tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

The assertions define the full contract. Key points:

**Before-state assertions** (already pass with the stub — must keep passing):
- `before.status === "Needs work"`
- `before.history === "Not visible"`
- `before.tasks === "0 owner tasks"`

**After-state assertions** (`after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)` where `now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z"`):
- `after.status === "Split-shipment exception active with replacement plan, owners, customer message, and audit trail."` (the exact `expectedOutcome` string)
- `after.owner === "Operations + Support + Finance + Logistics"` (ownerGroups joined with `" + "`)
- `after.nextStep.length > 8` (must set a non-trivial nextStep on workflow_state)
- `after.history === "Audit visible"` (needs a `customerVisible` audit event)
- `after.customerMessage.length > 12` (needs a persisted customer message body)
- `after.riskSummary === "6 signals: split, shipment, exception, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`
- `after.tasks === "4 owner tasks"`

**Persisted-state assertions** (`assertPersistedState`, reads the mutated `runData` directly):
- `db.workflow_state` must contain an item where `item.requestId === "order-split-shipme"` (`Boolean(state) === true`)
- `state.status === expectedStatus`
- `state.riskSignals.map(i=>i.name)` deep-equals the request's 6 signal names (in order) — so `workflow_state` must carry the full `riskSignals` array (objects with at least `name`)
- `db.owner_tasks` filtered by `requestId` has `length === 4`
- those tasks' `.ownerGroup` deep-equals `["Operations","Support","Finance","Logistics"]` (in that exact order)
- every task has truthy `title`, truthy `dueAt`, and `status === "open"`
- `db.customer_messages` has at least one item with matching `requestId` and a truthy `body`
- `db.audit_events` has at least one item with matching `requestId` and truthy `customerVisible`

So, to satisfy everything, `applyBenchmarkTask` must (for `request = db.workflow_requests[0]`):
1. Push to `workflow_state` an object with `{ requestId: request._id, title, status: request.expectedOutcome, nextStep, riskSignals: request.riskSignals }`.
2. Push 4 objects to `owner_tasks`, one per `request.ownerGroups` (preserving order), each with `{ requestId, ownerGroup, title (truthy), dueAt (truthy), status: "open" }`.
3. Push 1 object to `customer_messages` with `{ requestId, body (truthy, len > 12) }`.
4. Push 1 object to `audit_events` with `{ requestId, customerVisible: true }`.
5. Return `buildPortalView(db)`.

The `now` parameter is the natural source for `dueAt`/timestamps.

const TASK_DUE_HOURS = 4;

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const dueAt = new Date(new Date(now).getTime() + TASK_DUE_HOURS * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();

  db.workflow_state.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expectedOutcome,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    riskSignals: request.riskSignals
  });

  for (const ownerGroup of request.ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      requestId: request._id,
      ownerGroup,
      title: `${ownerGroup} action for ${request.title}`,
      dueAt,
      status: "open"
    });
  }

  db.customer_messages.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    body: request.customerMessage
  });

  db.audit_events.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    at: now,
    event: "workflow.reconciled",
    description: `${request.title} reconciled with replacement plan, owners, customer message, and audit trail.`,
    customerVisible: true
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

### 1. `tests/acceptance.test.mjs`
```js
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "Split-shipment exception active with replacement plan, owners, customer message, and audit trail.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: split-shipment-exception/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

### 2. `src/portal-view.mjs`
```js
export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

### 3. `src/workflow.mjs`

```js
export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  // TODO: Implement the benchmark workflow against the native data shape.
```

```json
}
```

### 5. `schema/schema.md`
```markdown
# Schema Contract: Split-shipment exception

Lane: postgres

Business prompt:
Implement a split-shipment exception workflow for a high-value order where partial fulfillment, carrier delay, inventory reservation, account tier, support case, and audit timeline must be reconciled.

Expected outcome:
Split-shipment exception active with replacement plan, owners, customer message, and audit trail.

Native data shape:
Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code.

Objects:
- benchmark_fixture: 1 table rows, fields: fixture_version, suite_id, task_id, generated_at
- accounts: 1 table rows, fields: account_id, name, tier, region
- account_contracts: 1 table rows, fields: contract_id, account_id, renewal_date, arr_cents, support_plan
- contacts: 2 table rows, fields: contact_id, account_id, role, email
- workflow_requests: 1 table rows, fields: request_id, task_id, account_id, title, primary_entity, expected_outcome, next_step, customer_message
- workflow_request_owner_groups: 4 table rows, fields: request_id, owner_group, group_order
- workflow_request_risk_signals: 6 table rows, fields: request_id, signal_name, detail, signal_order
- activities: 6 table rows, fields: activity_id, account_id, subject_id, summary, occurred_at
- workflow_state: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- owner_tasks: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- audit_events: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- customer_messages: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
```

```markdown
1. Load the workflow request.

3. Resolve owner groups and risk signals.
4. Persist workflow state, owner tasks, customer message, and audit event.

- Persist all required owner tasks.
- Persist one customer-safe message.

Fairness rule:
The lane may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data.
```

```js
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];

  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((group) => group.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);

  const signals = db.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((signal) => signal.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  // Reconcile the exception into a single workflow_state row.
  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    next_step: request.next_step,
    risk_signal_count: signals.length,
    updated_at: now
  });

  // One owner task per routed group, kept in escalation order.
  for (const group of ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      request_id: request.request_id,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: `${group.owner_group}: resolve split-shipment exception`,
      due_at: now,
      status: "open"
    });
  }

  // One customer-safe message reusing the approved copy.
  db.customer_messages.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    body: request.customer_message,
    created_at: now
  });

  // One customer-visible audit event closing the trail.
  db.audit_events.push({
    request_id: request.request_id,
    event: "split_shipment_exception_activated",
    detail: `Risk signals reconciled: ${signals.map((signal) => signal.signal_name).join(", ")}`,
    customer_visible: true,
    occurred_at: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

## What was implemented

The only change needed was implementing the `applyBenchmarkTask` stub in `src/workflow.mjs`. Everything else (the portal view, schema, fixtures, tests) was already in place and left untouched.

The function now reconciles the split-shipment exception against the normalized Postgres-style tables by:

1. **Loading the workflow request** and joining its owner groups (sorted by `group_order`) and risk signals (sorted by `signal_order`) — preserving the relational entity boundaries rather than flattening them.
2. **Persisting one `workflow_state` row** with the customer-facing status `"Split-shipment exception active with replacement plan, owners, customer message, and audit trail."`, the next step, and the risk-signal count.
3. **Persisting 4 `owner_tasks`** — one per routed group in escalation order (Operations → Support → Finance → Logistics), each with a `title`, `due_at`, and `status: "open"`.
4. **Persisting one customer-safe `customer_messages` row** reusing the approved copy.
5. **Persisting one customer-visible `audit_events` row** that closes the audit trail with the reconciled risk signals.

Then it returns `buildPortalView(db)` so the portal projection reflects the persisted state.

# Project Map: split-shipment-exception (postgres lane)

This is an **AST-Bench V1** benchmark target. It is a tiny, self-contained Node.js ESM project (no external dependencies, no real database server). The "postgres" lane is simulated entirely in-memory via a JSON fixture (`data/tables.json`) that mimics normalized relational tables. The task: implement the missing `applyBenchmarkTask` function in `src/workflow.mjs` so the acceptance test passes.

## 1. Directory tree (excluding node_modules, .git)

```
workspace/
├── AGENTS.md                          # Benchmark rules
├── README.md                          # Brief intro + run commands
├── package.json                       # ESM, no deps, test/render scripts

├── data/

    └── acceptance.test.mjs            # The acceptance test (do NOT modify)
```

No `dist/`, `build/`, `node_modules/`, lockfile, tsconfig, or `.env`. Git branch `master`, working tree clean. Only commit: `1a26785 Frozen before state`.

## 3. README / docs

**README.md** (full): States this is a frozen AST-Bench target; the workflow is intentionally missing; the acceptance test must fail before the agent implements `src/workflow.mjs`. Run `npm test` and `npm run render`.

**AGENTS.md** (full) — the binding rules:
- Implement only Split-shipment exception.
- Use the existing normalized table-shaped data in `data/`.
- Do not change tests. Do not change generated fixture data.
- Run `npm test` before finishing.
- Keep the result behavior-equivalent to the acceptance contract.

**schema/schema.md** — business prompt: "Implement a split-shipment exception workflow for a high-value order where partial fulfillment, carrier delay, inventory reservation, account tier, support case, and audit timeline must be reconciled." Expected outcome string: `"Split-shipment exception active with replacement plan, owners, customer message, and audit trail."` Lists all 12 objects/tables with field names and row counts (the four output tables — `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages` — start with 0 rows).

**schema/access-paths.md** — Primary entity: `order`. Read path: load workflow request → reconstruct context → resolve owner groups + risk signals → persist state/tasks/message/audit → render portal projection. Write path: persist one workflow state, all required owner tasks, one customer-safe message, one customer-visible audit event. Fairness rule: may stay DB-native but may not change tests or fixture data.

## 4. Database schema / migrations

**migrations/001_table_contract.sql** — Postgres `CREATE TABLE` statements for 12 tables. Note: this is a *contract* only; **the test never touches a real DB** — it loads `data/tables.json` instead. All columns are typed `text`. The four "output" tables are stubbed as a single `value text` column:

```sql
CREATE TABLE workflow_state    ( value text );
CREATE TABLE owner_tasks       ( value text );
CREATE TABLE audit_events      ( value text );
CREATE TABLE customer_messages ( value text );
```

Input tables: `benchmark_fixture`, `accounts`, `account_contracts`, `contacts`, `workflow_requests`, `workflow_request_owner_groups`, `workflow_request_risk_signals`, `activities` (full column lists in the file). No ORM, no models.

## 5. The acceptance test (full) — `tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

This is the spec. Key behavior it enforces (do NOT modify it):

- `now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z"`. Loads `data/tables.json`.
- **Before** (raw fixture through `buildPortalView`): `status === "Needs work"`, `history === "Not visible"`, `tasks === "0 owner tasks"`.
- Calls `applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)` → `after`. Then asserts on the returned view AND on the mutated `runData` tables:

Returned view assertions:
- `after.status === "Split-shipment exception active with replacement plan, owners, customer message, and audit trail."`
- `after.owner === expectedOwners.join(" + ")` where `expectedOwners` = owner groups sorted by `group_order` → `"Operations + Support + Finance + Logistics"`.
- `after.nextStep.length > 8` (truthy non-trivial string).
- `after.history === "Audit visible"`.
- `after.customerMessage.length > 12`.
- `after.riskSummary === "6 signals: split, shipment, exception, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"` (signals sorted by `signal_order`).
- `after.tasks === "4 owner tasks"`.

Persisted-state assertions (`assertPersistedState`) — `applyBenchmarkTask` must **mutate `runData` in place**:
- `workflow_state` has a row matching `request_id` with `.status === expectedStatus`.
- `owner_tasks` has exactly 4 rows (one per owner group, in `group_order`), each with truthy `title`, `due_at`, and `status === "open"`; `owner_group` order must `deepStrictEqual` `["Operations","Support","Finance","Logistics"]`.
- `customer_messages` has a row for the request with a truthy `body`.
- `audit_events` has a row for the request with `customer_visible` truthy.

The `request_id` in the fixture is `"order-split-shipme"` (note: truncated, no trailing "nt").

## 6. Source code related to orders / workflows / audit

**`src/workflow.mjs`** — THE TARGET. Currently a stub that just returns the before-view:
```js
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";
export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  // TODO: Implement the benchmark workflow against the native data shape.
  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```
Must be implemented to: read `workflow_requests[0]`, resolve owner groups (sorted by `group_order`) and risk signals (sorted by `signal_order`), then **push** rows into `db.workflow_state`, `db.owner_tasks`, `db.audit_events`, `db.customer_messages`, and finally return `buildPortalView(db)` (which will then reflect the persisted state).

**`src/portal-view.mjs`** — Pure read-only projection used by both before/after and by the test. Given the tables object, finds the first request, looks up `workflow_state` by `request_id`, filters `owner_tasks`/`audit_events`/`customer_messages`/`risk_signals` by `request_id`, and returns a view object with keys: `taskId, title, status, owner, nextStep, riskSummary, tasks, history, customerMessage`. It defaults to `"Needs work"` / `"Not visible"` / `"0 owner tasks"` when output tables are empty. **This is the contract that `applyBenchmarkTask` must satisfy by populating the tables it reads.** Key detail: `riskSummary` is computed here as `` `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map(s => s.signal_name).join(", ")}` `` but only when a `state` row exists (otherwise `"Risk not scored"`) — so persisting `workflow_state` is what unlocks the risk summary.

**`src/render-proof.mjs`** — Non-test artifact generator. Builds `before` and `after` views and writes `artifacts/before-after.json` and an SVG diff table. Not involved in passing the test, but `npm run render` should not crash after implementation.

## 7. Database connection / config

**None.** There is no DB driver (`pg`/`postgres`/`knex`/Prisma), no connection pool, no env vars, no `.env`. The "database" is `data/tables.json`, read synchronously via `readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url))` and `structuredClone`d. The migration SQL is a descriptive contract that is never executed. "Persistence" = mutating the in-memory cloned object's arrays.

## 8. Seed / fixture data

**`data/tables.json`** (161 lines) — the single fixture, structured as one JSON object whose keys are table names mapping to arrays of row objects:

- `benchmark_fixture`: 1 row (version/suite/task/generated_at).
- `accounts`: 1 row — `acct-split-ship`, name "Order Account 1", tier `enterprise`, region `NA`.
- `account_contracts`: 1 row — platinum support, arr_cents 7200000, renewal 2026-09-30.
- `contacts`: 2 rows — Operations + Finance contacts.
- `workflow_requests`: 1 row — `request_id: "order-split-shipme"`, `task_id: "split-shipment-exception"`, `primary_entity: "order"`, plus pre-written `expected_outcome`, `next_step` ("Operations owner review by 16:00"), and `customer_message` ("Split-shipment exception is being handled by Operations, Support, Finance, Logistics.").
- `workflow_request_owner_groups`: 4 rows — Operations(1), Support(2), Finance(3), Logistics(4).
- `workflow_request_risk_signals`: 6 rows — split(1), shipment(2), exception(3), enterprise account(4), open customer impact(5), audit required(6), each with a `detail` string.
- `activities`: 6 rows — timeline entries 2026-06-10..06-15, mirroring the 6 signals.
- `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages`: **empty arrays `[]`** — these are what the implementation must fill.

Sample row (`workflow_request_owner_groups`):
```json
{ "request_id": "order-split-shipme", "owner_group": "Operations", "group_order": 1 }
```

The fixture conveniently already contains the exact `next_step` and `customer_message` strings on the request row, which the implementation can reuse to populate `workflow_state.next_step` and the `customer_messages.body`.

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/split-shipment-exception/claude-code/repeat-1/postgres/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:105:{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_bdrk_zguzlsix2xyixdetizfpwkxmikx3cihd3mlvcfilfeqsozwa567a","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_bdrk_01DbpXZ9VpN6yKyfe8qszwGN","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/split-shipment-exception/claude-code/repeat-1/postgres/workspace/src/workflow.mjs","old_string":"export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {

}","new_string":"export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const requestId = request.request_id;

  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === requestId)
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);

  const dueAt = now || new Date().toISOString();

  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    next_step: request.next_step
  });

  for (const group of ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      request_id: requestId,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: `${group.owner_group} review: ${request.title}`,
      due_at: dueAt,
      status: "open"
    });
  }

  db.customer_messages.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    body: request.customer_message,
    created_at: dueAt
  });

  db.audit_events.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    event: "split-shipment-exception-active",
    customer_visible: true,
    occurred_at: dueAt
  });

```javascript
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const requestId = request.request_id;

  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === requestId)
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);

  const dueAt = now || new Date().toISOString();

  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    next_step: request.next_step
  });

  for (const group of ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      request_id: requestId,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: `${group.owner_group} review: ${request.title}`,
      due_at: dueAt,
      status: "open"
    });
  }

  db.customer_messages.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    body: request.customer_message,
    created_at: dueAt
  });

  db.audit_events.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    event: "split-shipment-exception-active",
    customer_visible: true,
    occurred_at: dueAt
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

11	strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

20	strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
21	strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
22	strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
23	strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
24	strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
25	strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
26	assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

30	function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
31	  
32	  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
33	  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
34	  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
35	  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
36	  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");
37	  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
38	  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
39	  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
40	  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.dueAt && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
41	  strictEqual(db.customer_messages.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");

```js
export function buildPortalView(db) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

  return {
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

This is the critical contract: `buildPortalView` reads from these collections, keyed by `requestId === request._id` (where `request._id` is `"case-sla-breach-r"`). The implementation in `workflow.mjs` must write documents whose `requestId` matches `db.workflow_requests[0]._id`.

### 6. Source structure & "MongoDB integration"

There are no controllers/routes/services in the web-framework sense. The architecture is a minimal functional pipeline:

- **`src/workflow.mjs`** — the command/write side (the stub to implement). Signature: `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)`.
- **`src/portal-view.mjs`** — the query/read side (the projection). Frozen contract.
- **`src/render-proof.mjs`** — a reporting script (`npm run render`) that diffs before/after and writes `artifacts/before-after.json` and `.svg`.
- **`tests/acceptance.test.mjs`** — the single acceptance gate.

**"MongoDB" integration:** This lane is *MongoDB-shaped* but not MongoDB-connected. The "database" is the in-memory object parsed from `data/collections.json` — an object whose keys are MongoDB-style collection names (`workflow_requests`, `owner_tasks`, etc.), each an array of documents with `_id`/`requestId` fields. Collections are queried with JS array methods (`.find`, `.filter`, `.some`). The MongoDB MCP server is connected in this environment but the test does not touch it. The task is purely to mutate the in-memory `db` object.

### What needs to happen to pass (derived from test + portal-view contract)

`applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` must, before returning `buildPortalView(db)`:
1. Push one doc into `db.workflow_state` with `{ requestId: "case-sla-breach-r", status: <expectedOutcome>, nextStep: <non-empty, len>8>, riskSignals: <the 5 signals, names in order>, title: ... }`.
2. Push 4 docs into `db.owner_tasks`, one per `ownerGroup` in order `["Support","Customer Success","Product","Operations"]`, each with truthy `title`, truthy `dueAt`, and `status: "open"`, plus `requestId`.
3. Push one doc into `db.customer_messages` with `requestId` and a `body` of length > 12.
4. Push one doc into `db.audit_events` with `requestId` and `customerVisible: true`.
5. Return the portal view (the existing return of `buildPortalView(db)` will then reflect all the above).

Note the ordering coupling: `after.owner` must equal `ownerGroups.join(" + ")` and `buildPortalView` builds `owner` from `owner_tasks.map(t => t.ownerGroup).join(" + ")`, so owner tasks must be pushed in the exact `ownerGroups` array order. Same for `riskSignals` names order in `workflow_state`.

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const startedAt = new Date(now);
  const dueAt = new Date(startedAt.getTime() + 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();

  db.workflow_state.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    status: request.expectedOutcome,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    riskSignals: request.riskSignals,
    title: request.title
  });

  for (const ownerGroup of request.ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      requestId: request._id,
      ownerGroup,
      title: `${ownerGroup} review for ${request.title}`,
      dueAt,
      status: "open"
    });
  }

  db.customer_messages.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    body: request.customerMessage
  });

  db.audit_events.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    customerVisible: true,
    at: startedAt.toISOString(),
    description: `SLA response timer started for ${request.title}; escalation routed to ${request.ownerGroups.join(", ")}.`
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

```js
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const startedAt = new Date(now);
  const dueAt = new Date(startedAt.getTime() + 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();

  db.workflow_state.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    status: request.expectedOutcome,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    riskSignals: request.riskSignals,
    title: request.title
  });

  for (const ownerGroup of request.ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      requestId: request._id,
      ownerGroup,
      title: `${ownerGroup} review for ${request.title}`,
      dueAt,
      status: "open"
    });
  }

  db.customer_messages.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    body: request.customerMessage
  });

  db.audit_events.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    customerVisible: true,
    at: startedAt.toISOString(),
    description: `SLA response timer started for ${request.title}; escalation routed to ${request.ownerGroups.join(", ")}.`
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/data-access-audit-export/claude-code/repeat-1/postgres/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:51:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01UrTWuz7LvTBTsHZQycpvmB","type":"tool_result","content":"1	import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
2	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
3	import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
4	import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";
5	
6	const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
7	const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
8	const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
9	strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
10	strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
11	strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");
12	
13	const runData = structuredClone(data);
14	const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
15	const expectedStatus = "Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail.";
16	const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
17	const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));
18	
19	strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
20	strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
21	strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
22	strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
23	strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
24	strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
25	strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
26	assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);
27	
28	console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: data-access-audit-export/postgres");
29	
30	function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
31	  
32	  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
33	  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
34	  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
35	  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
36	  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
37	  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
38	  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
39	  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
40	  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
41	  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
42	}
43	"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01Waruct9y5TDp7P1u19Prvb","session_id":"9fafce4b-4166-4fa3-9962-c64de5491e91","uuid":"51d14004-ac0a-4e28-9773-a8366c0551ee","timestamp":"2026-06-23T01:58:38.497Z","subagent_type":"worker","task_description":"Explore workspace structure"}

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/data-access-audit-export/claude-code/repeat-1/postgres/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:61:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01JY5quMiPVMfhnWmtmPQmKZ","type":"tool_result","content":"1	# Schema Contract: Data-access audit export

3	Lane: postgres

5	Business prompt:
6	Implement a data-access audit export workflow for a regulated account. Combine user access, account records, support cases, order events, compliance reviews, approval history, and export status.
7	
8	Expected outcome:
9	Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail.
10	
11	Native data shape:
12	Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code.
13	
14	Objects:
15	- benchmark_fixture: 1 table rows, fields: fixture_version, suite_id, task_id, generated_at
16	- accounts: 1 table rows, fields: account_id, name, tier, region
17	- account_contracts: 1 table rows, fields: contract_id, account_id, renewal_date, arr_cents, support_plan
18	- contacts: 2 table rows, fields: contact_id, account_id, role, email
19	- workflow_requests: 1 table rows, fields: request_id, task_id, account_id, title, primary_entity, expected_outcome, next_step, customer_message
20	- workflow_request_owner_groups: 4 table rows, fields: request_id, owner_group, group_order
21	- workflow_request_risk_signals: 6 table rows, fields: request_id, signal_name, detail, signal_order
22	- activities: 6 table rows, fields: activity_id, account_id, subject_id, summary, occurred_at
23	- workflow_state: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
24	- owner_tasks: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
25	- audit_events: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
26	- customer_messages: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
27	"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01Waruct9y5TDp7P1u19Prvb","session_id":"9fafce4b-4166-4fa3-9962-c64de5491e91","uuid":"18226fc8-f279-451b-be69-79dd1d721878","timestamp":"2026-06-23T01:58:41.538Z","subagent_type":"worker","task_description":"Explore workspace structure"}

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/data-access-audit-export/claude-code/repeat-1/postgres/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:66:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01QZiJnZTcSFHnyGU12HwCcd","type":"tool_result","content":"1	export function buildPortalView(tables) {
2	  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
3	  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
4	  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
5	  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
6	  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
7	  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
8	    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
9	    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);
10	
11	  return {
12	    taskId: request.task_id,
13	    title: state?.title || request.title,
14	    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
15	    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
16	    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
17	    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
18	    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
19	    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
20	    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
21	  };
22	}
23	"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01Waruct9y5TDp7P1u19Prvb","session_id":"9fafce4b-4166-4fa3-9962-c64de5491e91","uuid":"24e6891c-c549-4237-ad1c-ccf6e5a2d63c","timestamp":"2026-06-23T01:58:42.524Z","subagent_type":"worker","task_description":"Explore workspace structure"}

/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/data-access-audit-export/claude-code/repeat-1/postgres/raw-transcript/claude-code.jsonl:81:{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_012yMrLkfHCygcJEUNqsYTRy","type":"tool_result","content":"1	import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
2	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
3	import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
4	import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";
5	
6	const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
7	const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
8	const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
9	strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
10	strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
11	strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");
12	
13	const runData = structuredClone(data);
14	const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
15	const expectedStatus = "Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail.";
16	const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
17	const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));
18	
19	strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
20	strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
21	strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
22	strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
23	strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
24	strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
25	strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
26	assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);
27	
28	console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: data-access-audit-export/postgres");
29	
30	function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
31	  
32	  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
33	  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
34	  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
35	  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
36	  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
37	  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
38	  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
39	  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
40	  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
41	  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
42	}
43	"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_01Pk9vgcQ9gMz9GB98jBUtAC","session_id":"9fafce4b-4166-4fa3-9962-c64de5491e91","uuid":"1a3033a3-72b3-402e-86af-a0a3df042fef","timestamp":"2026-06-23T01:58:46.826Z","subagent_type":"worker","task_description":"Read test files in detail"}

## File path: `/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs/ast-bench-v1/data-access-audit-export/claude-code/repeat-1/postgres/workspace/tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

```javascript
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: data-access-audit-export/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

```js
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: data-access-audit-export/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

```js
export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

Key insight: `buildPortalView` derives the return shape. `applyBenchmarkTask` must write rows into `db.workflow_state`, `db.owner_tasks`, `db.audit_events`, `db.customer_messages` so that the recomputed portal view matches. For `riskSummary` to differ from before, a `state` row must exist (the summary is gated on `state` being present). For `nextStep > 8`, the persisted state row should carry `next_step` (request value `"Compliance owner review by 16:00"` works).

**`src/render-proof.mjs`** (full — produces `artifacts/before-after.json` and `.svg`; not under test, run via `npm run render`): loads `data/tables.json`, computes `before` = `buildPortalView(clone)`, `after` = `applyBenchmarkTask(clone, now)`, writes both to `artifacts/`. ## `src/portal-view.mjs`
```javascript
export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

```javascript
import { mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "./workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);

mkdirSync("artifacts", { recursive: true });
writeFileSync("artifacts/before-after.json", JSON.stringify({ lane: "postgres", before, after }, null, 2) + "\n");
writeFileSync("artifacts/before-after.svg", svg({ before, after }));

function svg({ before, after }) {
  const rows = [
    ["Status", before.status, after.status],
    ["Owner", before.owner, after.owner],
    ["Next", before.nextStep, after.nextStep],
    ["Risk", before.riskSummary, after.riskSummary],
    ["Tasks", before.tasks, after.tasks],
    ["Audit", before.history, after.history]
  ];
  const esc = (value) => String(value).replaceAll("&", "&").replaceAll("<", "<").replaceAll(">", ">");
  const body = rows.map((row, index) => {
    const y = 160 + index * 58;
    return `<text x="52" y="${y}" class="k">${esc(row[0])}</text><text x="180" y="${y}" class="v">${esc(row[1])}</text><text x="620" y="${y}" class="g">${esc(row[2])}</text>`;
  }).join("");
  return `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1080" height="560" viewBox="0 0 1080 560">
    <style>.bg{fill:#0f1412}.card{fill:#f5f1e8;stroke:#d9d2bf}.h{font:800 28px system-ui;fill:#f5f1e8}.s{font:600 14px system-ui;fill:#b9c1b1}.k{font:700 14px system-ui;fill:#5a6256}.v{font:650 14px system-ui;fill:#171b17}.g{font:750 14px system-ui;fill:#087a4a}.line{stroke:#d9d2bf}</style>
    <rect class="bg" width="1080" height="560"/>
    <text x="40" y="50" class="h">AST-Bench Workflow State</text>
    <text x="40" y="78" class="s">postgres target / deterministic fixture / captured by render script</text>
    <rect x="36" y="104" width="1008" height="390" rx="6" class="card"/>
    <text x="180" y="124" class="k">Before</text><text x="620" y="124" class="k">After</text>
    <line x1="150" y1="134" x2="150" y2="470" class="line"/><line x1="590" y1="134" x2="590" y2="470" class="line"/>
    ${body}
  </svg>`;
}
```

```sql
);

);

);

);

);

);

);

  value text
);
```

## `schema/schema.md`
```markdown
# Schema Contract: Data-access audit export

Lane: postgres

Business prompt:
Implement a data-access audit export workflow for a regulated account. Combine user access, account records, support cases, order events, compliance reviews, approval history, and export status.

Expected outcome:
Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail.

Native data shape:
Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code.

Objects:
- benchmark_fixture: 1 table rows, fields: fixture_version, suite_id, task_id, generated_at
- accounts: 1 table rows, fields: account_id, name, tier, region
- account_contracts: 1 table rows, fields: contract_id, account_id, renewal_date, arr_cents, support_plan
- contacts: 2 table rows, fields: contact_id, account_id, role, email
- workflow_requests: 1 table rows, fields: request_id, task_id, account_id, title, primary_entity, expected_outcome, next_step, customer_message
- workflow_request_owner_groups: 4 table rows, fields: request_id, owner_group, group_order
- workflow_request_risk_signals: 6 table rows, fields: request_id, signal_name, detail, signal_order
- activities: 6 table rows, fields: activity_id, account_id, subject_id, summary, occurred_at
- workflow_state: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- owner_tasks: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- audit_events: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- customer_messages: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
```

## `schema/access-paths.md`
```markdown
# Access Paths: Data-access audit export

The coding agent must preserve the same user-visible behavior in both lanes.

Primary entity: audit

Read path:
1. Load the workflow request.
2. Reconstruct account or subject context from benchmark_fixture, accounts, account_contracts, contacts, workflow_requests, workflow_request_owner_groups, workflow_request_risk_signals, activities, workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages.
3. Resolve owner groups and risk signals.
4. Persist workflow state, owner tasks, customer message, and audit event.
5. Render the portal projection.

Write path:
- Persist one workflow state.
- Persist all required owner tasks.
- Persist one customer-safe message.
- Persist one customer-visible audit event.

Fairness rule:
The lane may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data.
```

## `tests/acceptance.test.mjs`
```javascript
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "Audit export ready with scoped records, approvers, customer-safe summary, and hashable audit trail.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: data-access-audit-export/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const requestId = request.request_id;

  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === requestId)
    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);

  const riskSignals = db.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === requestId)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  // Scoped-record context pulled from the native data shape (real fields only).
  const accountId = request.account_id;
  const activityCount = db.activities.filter(
    (item) => item.subject_id === requestId || item.account_id === accountId
  ).length;
  const contractPlan = db.account_contracts.find((item) => item.account_id === accountId)?.support_plan ?? null;

  // Idempotent: drop any prior rows for this request before re-persisting.
  db.workflow_state = db.workflow_state.filter((item) => item.request_id !== requestId);
  db.owner_tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id !== requestId);
  db.customer_messages = db.customer_messages.filter((item) => item.request_id !== requestId);
  db.audit_events = db.audit_events.filter((item) => item.request_id !== requestId);

  db.workflow_state.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    task_id: request.task_id,
    account_id: accountId,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    next_step: request.next_step,
    risk_signal_count: riskSignals.length,
    owner_group_count: ownerGroups.length,
    updated_at: now
  });

  // 24h SLA deadline, deterministic from `now`.
  const dueAt = new Date(new Date(now).getTime() + 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();
  for (const group of ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      request_id: requestId,
      owner_group: group.owner_group,
      title: `${group.owner_group} review for ${request.title}`,
      due_at: dueAt,
      status: "open"
    });
  }

  db.customer_messages.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    channel: "portal",
    body: request.customer_message,
    customer_visible: true,
    created_at: now
  });

  const payload = {
    request_id: requestId,
    task_id: request.task_id,
    account_id: accountId,
    status: request.expected_outcome,
    next_step: request.next_step,
    owners: ownerGroups.map((item) => item.owner_group),
    risk_signals: riskSignals.map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail })),
    scoped_records: {
      account_id: accountId,
      support_plan: contractPlan,
      activity_count: activityCount
    }
  };
  const hash = createHash("sha256").update(JSON.stringify(payload)).digest("hex");

  db.audit_events.push({
    request_id: requestId,
    event_type: "audit_export_ready",
    summary: `Audit export ready for ${request.title}: ${ownerGroups.length} approvers, ${riskSignals.length} risk signals.`,
    customer_visible: true,
    payload,
    hash,
    created_at: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}

2	import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";
